allowed: explicit size gates and optional hardware blockers

gates.evidence: all protocol sizes and baseline columns have a planning row
gates.name: full_grid_enumerated

gates.evidence: required rows are classified as measured, ready, or size-gated
gates.name: required_rows_classified

gates.evidence: planner does not fabricate heavy benchmark rows
gates.name: full_campaign_not_executed

gates.evidence: no full benchmark matrix or QPU rows exist
gates.name: advantage_claim_blocked

acceptance: All required classical and simulator columns either contain measured rows or explicit size-gated skips.
Every timing row records command, machine, dependency versions, and git commit.
MPS/TN diagnostics are present before any advantage-language figure is promoted.
Hardware rows are optional until credits are approved and must never be extrapolated as broad advantage.

baselines.claim_boundary: Classical oscillator ODE baseline; not a Hilbert-space solver.
baselines.label: classical_ode

baselines.claim_boundary: Dense exact diagonalisation is skipped by memory gate above its cap.
baselines.label: dense_eigh

baselines.claim_boundary: Sparse eigensolver timing is not equivalent to full dynamics.
baselines.label: sparse_eigsh

baselines.claim_boundary: MPS results bound classical spoofability; low entanglement weakens advantage claims.
baselines.label: mps_tensor_network

baselines.claim_boundary: GPU dense timings are classical-validation support, not a quantum baseline.
baselines.label: gpu_dense_reference

baselines.claim_boundary: Aer statevector is a simulator baseline, not hardware evidence.
baselines.label: aer_statevector

baselines.claim_boundary: Hardware column is evidence only for submitted preregistered sizes; absence of IBM credits must degrade gracefully without broad advantage claims.
baselines.label: qpu_hardware

protocol.claim_boundary: S2 may publish scaling, memory, and spoofability boundaries. It must not claim broad quantum advantage without preregistered hardware data and classical tensor-network baselines at the same problem family.
falsification: If MPS discarded weight remains small at target sizes, any strong quantum-advantage framing is rejected.
If Aer/statevector or sparse classical baselines dominate the proposed quantum path, crossover language is rejected.
If hardware rows are unavailable or noisy, the result remains a classical scaling study only.
